items.title: Joseph Aoun in Washington: Bilal Saab firmly believes 'this meeting couldn't have gone any better'
items.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Bilal Saab, Senior Managing Director of the Trends Research Group and a close friend of Lebanese President Joseph Aoun. Mr. Saab offers a cautiously optimistic assessment of a pivotal moment in Lebanese diplomacy, arguing that President Aoun's visit to Washington represents an important opportunity to consolidate international backing for Lebanon's sovereignty and state institutions. While acknowledging the formidable structural challenges posed by Hezbollah, regional tensions, and extremely complex Israeli-Lebanese security challenges, Mr. Saab contends that success will not come from military coercion, but rather a focus on the gradual strengthening of the Lebanese state. According to Mr. Saab, Lebanon is both a testing ground for American regional diplomacy and a fragile experiment in state-building that will require sustained political support and not symbolic declarations.

The President removed Fedorov from his post last week, citing a clash between the two men. The protests have brought Syrsky’s unpopularity to the forefront. Through credited with stopping the Russian invaders at the gates of Kyiv in 2022, he’s since gained a reputation for brutality, and for wasting lives unnecessarily. items.title: Milei almost halved education and science spending, reports finds
items.description: University of Buenos Aires researchers say education investment has almost halved in two years since President Javier Milei took office, while second Grupo EPC analysis says funding for science has fallen by more than 46%. Leer más

items.title: Yemen's Iranian-backed Houthis say they will block Saudi shipping at Red Sea gateway
items.description: Bab el-Mandeb, at the southern tip of the Arabian Peninsula, is the gateway to the Red Sea through which around 12% of the world’s trade usually passes

items.title: Meta adds Xbox Game Pass starter to Horizon+ subscription
Trump orders new 50% tariff on many Canadian goods
items.description: The tariffs take effect in 30 days and cover various items including wine, hockey sticks and cement, said the White House

items.title: Trump allows direct US flights to Lebanon in historic move
items.description: It comes over 40 years after the US suspended the direct route with Lebanon. The announcement was made after US President Donald Trump met with Lebanese President Joseph Aoun.

items.title: 'Works every time': Superstition ramps up ahead in Argentina of World Cup final
items.description: Football fans are putting their faith in good luck rituals ahead of Sunday's World Cup final. But these wacky habits are no joke to those who practice them. Leer más

items.title: Senate debate on ruling party’s land ownership bill pushed back after spat
items.description: Milei government fails to gather votes, postpones upper house debate over land ownership reform bill; Fourth delay for controversial bill that would eliminate restrictions on the foreign purchase of rural land; Comes after spat between Vice-President Villarruel and La Libertad Avanza Senate chief Patricia Bullrich. Leer más

items.title: Ten killed as Russian attacks on merchant ships in Black Sea intensify
items.description: Russian strikes on the Odesa region have killed 28 people in July alone, local authorities say.

items.title: French parliament backs social media ban for under-15s in EU first
items.description: FRANCE 24's Clovis Casali reports from France's National Assembly, where lawmakers have approved a ban on social media for children and teenagers under-15, the first EU country to do so. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Philippe Coen, lawyer and founder of the NGO Respect Zone.

items.title: The war in Iran has left hundreds of U.S. diplomats in limbo
items.description: As the U.S-Iran war ramps up again, hundreds of diplomats are in limbo. The State Department drew down staffing due to Iran's attacks on Gulf states. It's unclear if they'll return to normal business.

items.title: Russia's largest ballistic assault of war rocks Kyiv
items.description: Fires broke out across the Ukrainian capital after Russia's latest missile attack, which used the highest number of ballistic missiles of the war so far. Several people have been injured in Kyiv.

items.title: Former Socceroos captain Paul Wade reveals probable CTE diagnosis
items.description: Paul Wade says he wants to share his experience to raise awareness about concussion, repeated head impacts and brain health across all levels of football.

items.title: Anthropic sued for infringing neural network technology patents
Nigeria and Ghana call on AU to address 'Afrophobic protests' in South Africa
items.description: Nigeria and Ghana have condemned what they called the recent "Afrophobic protests targeting African nationals in South Africa." In a statement, Nigeria's ministry of foreign affairs said the two countries want the issue placed on the agenda at the next African Union summit.

items.title: China narrows AI gap, challenges US influence
items.description: At the World Artificial Intelligence Conference in Shanghai, Beijing showcased advances in AI while promoting its vision of open-source development and global governance.

items.title: Lebanese army says troops deploying in 'pilot zone' after Israeli withdrawal
items.description: A US-brokered agreement should see Israeli troops pulling out of a small part of Lebanon occupied during their conflict with Hezbollah.

items.title: Gabon's President Oligui Ngema arrives in France for state visit
items.description: Gabon's President Brice Oligui Ngema kicked off a state visit to France on Monday, a follow up to French President Macron's visit to Libreville last year.

items.title: Ghana court jails opposition party's regional chairman for illegal mining
items.description: A Ghanaian High Court has sentenced Bernard Antwi Boasiako, popularly known as Chairman Wontumi, to 20 years in prison with hard labour for illegal mining.

items.title: World Breastfeeding Week: FG urges stronger support for breastfeeding
items.description: The global event, observed annually from 1 to 7 August, will be commemorated this year under the theme, “Breastfeeding for a Sustainable Start in Life: Strengthen What Works.” The post World Breastfeeding Week: FG urges stronger support for breastfeeding appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Andy Burnham becomes U.K. PM, promising new social and political model
items.description: ‘I go with good grace and a smile on my face’, says outgoing Prime Minister Keir Starmer says

items.title: Beyond Spain's win: the moments that defined the 2026 FIFA World Cup
items.description: The final whistle may have crowned Spain as world champions, but the 2026 FIFA World Cup will be remembered for far more than who lifted the trophy.

items.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's new prime minister
items.description: Andy Burnham has been sworn in as the successor to British Prime Minister Keir Starmer. Burnham, the former Manchester mayor, has become a leading figure in the Labour Party and one of the UK's most popular politicians.

items.title: How one of Iran's 'Axis' allies is threatening to widen the war
items.description: The Houthi rebels, described as one of Iran's "merchants of chaos", have threatened to widen the conflict in the Middle East by declaring a maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia. Here's what could happen next.

items.title: Messi eyes glorious farewell as Spain, Argentina clash in World Cup final
items.description: Holders Argentina are bidding to become the first team in 64 years to successfully defend the title, in what likely to be the final World Cup match of captain Lionel Messi's career. Leer más

items.title: Spain: Emergency crews battle wildfire north of Madrid
items.description: More than 25 firefighting planes have been deployed to battle the blaze in central Spain, which has been raging for three days.

items.title: ‘All I think about is her’: Families demand answers as Nigeria's boat disasters keep happening
items.description: Hundreds of lives are lost each year in Nigeria as a result of boat accidents yet nothing seems to change.

items.title: Flash floods kill at least 20 in Afghanistan and leave more than 100 missing
items.description: “Unfortunately, severe floods today in Parun city and several other areas of Nuristan Province have caused heavy loss of life and property among our fellow citizens,” says Afghan authorities

items.title: Gaborone accuses Moscow of luring Batswana to battlefield
items.description: Botswana is the latest nation to accuse Moscow of using deceptive tactics to lure foreign recruits to the battlefield. Without saying how many of its citizens were involved, Gaborone's foreign ministry claimed its nationals were duped into going to Russia and then forced into active combat.

items.title: ‘Everyone's afraid to die’: Struggle to recruit soldiers in Ukraine turns violent
items.description: Aggression against recruitment officers has become increasingly threatening, police say.

items.title: Iran hits out at Gulf neighbours as U.S. presses on with strikes
items.description: Oil prices surged to a one-month high as the United States and Iran tussled for control of the vital strait, with Brent crude rising above $91 a barrel — the highest price since June 11

items.title: Argentina's World Cup heroics move me to tears, says goalkeeper Emiliano Martínez
items.description: Argentina keeper says he is enjoying this World Cup campaign much more than in 2022, despite coming into the tournament injured. Leer más

items.title: Kuwaitis ration electricity after Iranian strikes hit power grid
items.description: Kuwaitis are rationing electricity after Iranian strikes on power infrastructure are raising fears of outages

items.title: From hundreds to thousands: How India's 'cockroach' protest movement has grown
items.description: BBC's Azadeh Moshiri reports as thousands attempt to march to India's parliament in support of the Cockroach Janta Party's demand for education reforms.

items.title: U.S. funding cuts have gutted global AIDS program, report finds
items.description: Clinics closed, staff laid off as PEPFAR disruptions hit dozens of countries

items.title: ‘Our bodies contain poison’: Landmark trial in Argentina probes dangers of agrochemicals
items.description: Trial over agrochemical spraying in the city of Pergamino has set an unprecedented legal benchmark: for the first time, a federal court placed the entire agricultural production chain in the dock, from farmers and agronomists to public officials. Trial ends in acquittal for nearly all those excused, except two municipal employees. Leer más

items.title: South Africa signs $1.5bn World Bank loan for infrastructure development
items.description: It will help the country confront challenges in electricity supply, freight transport, and water and sanitation services that have long constrained economic growth

items.title: Germany: CDU's Jens Spahn resigns amid surrogacy controversy
items.description: Surrogacy is not permitted in Germany and the conservative Christian Democratic Union (CDU) opposes its legalization. Spahn and his husband had their child through a surrogate mother in the US.

items.title: Hiding behind the World Cup, Milei siblings restructure their government
items.description: The World Cup provided the Milei administration a much-needed breather after three months under siege. Javier and Karina Milei are learning by doing, but by doing so they are slowly but surely resembling what they initially promised to battle. Leer más

items.title: Africa surpasses Asia with highest population facing hunger: UN
items.description: The UN's annual report on food security and nutrition found that there has been gradual improvement towards reducing hunger overall in recent years, but that progress has been uneven

items.title: Milei says won't attend World Cup final out of superstition
items.description: British calls on FIFA to investigate after Argentina's players held up a banner reading "Las Malvinas son Argentinas" after their victory over England. Leer más

items.title: Master and apprentice as Spain, Argentina coaches meet in World Cup final
items.description: Nine years ago, Argentina's coach was just another former professional player pursuing a career in coaching, studying for his pro-licence on the outskirts of Madrid. His teacher on the course? Luis de la Fuente. Leer más

items.title: Iran confirms receiving proposals from mediators amid war with US
items.description: Iran said Monday it has received proposals from mediators as back-and-forth attacks with the United States continue.

items.title: Australia's Origin Energy probes potential unauthorised data access
Crush at Burundi prayer meeting leaves eight dead
items.description: A packed evangelical service in Bujumbura turned deadly on Saturday night, when a crowd crush killed eight people and left nine hurt.

items.title: Former Canadian finance minister on Trump's tariff plan and its impact
items.description: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Chrystia Freeland, former Canadian Finance Minister, about the potential impact of President Trump's proposed tariffs.

items.title: Extreme heat and weather conditions attributed to stagnant jet stream
items.description: It’s no coincidence that extreme heat is engulfing huge swaths of Asia, Europe and North America all at the same time.

items.title: Six jailed Ivory Coast opposition party officials freed after months in detention
items.description: The Abidjan PDCI officials, arrested in October last year, were released on Monday after nine months in detention.

items.description: Press reflects on Messi's emotional farewell and an uncertain future; Headlines focus on tears, farewell fears and the end of an era. Leer más

items.title: 'Massive' drop in tourists this winter hits WA's south
items.description: Despite being an idyllic location for a winter getaway, businesses in WA's Great Southern have recorded a decline in tourists this season.

items.title: Boy, 13, dies during World Cup celebrations in Spain
items.description: Two other young people were injured after a fountain partially collapsed in Salamanca's Ciudad Rodrigo.
